programme.title: I Trapped the Devil
programme.desc: Hoping for a joyful family reunion, Matt and his wife pay a surprise visit to the home of his estranged brother, Steve, to celebrate Christmas. To their shock, they soon learn that Steve has a hostage in his basement -- a man he claims is the devil.

programme.title: Nobody Gets Out Alive
programme.desc: Hunter's (Brian Gallagher) life turns upside down after his daughter is killed by a drunken driver. He disappears into the woods, and no one sees him for years -- until several partying teenagers are brutally killed one by one.

programme.title: Deadsight
programme.desc: A pregnant police officer and a blind man fight for their lives against swarms of the living dead.

programme.title: The Shed
programme.desc: A teen and his best friend endure nonstop torment from bullies at school, but that soon changes when one discovers a bloodthirsty creature that resides in a country shed.

programme.title: Dementia
programme.desc: Hired as a live-in nurse, a seemingly sweet woman (Kristina Klebe) unleashes her dark side as she torments a Vietnam War veteran (Gene Jones) who has dementia.

programme.title: The Babadook
programme.desc: A troubled widow (Essie Davis) discovers that her son is telling the truth about a monster that entered their home through the pages of a children's book.

programme.title: Feral
programme.desc: A wild animal attacks six grad students on a weekend hike in the woods. One by one, they become infected with an unknown virus that turns them into bloodthirsty creatures.

programme.title: Hatching
programme.desc: After finding a wounded bird, a girl brings its strange egg home and nurtures it until it hatches. The creature that emerges soon becomes her closest friend and a living nightmare, plunging her into a twisted reality that her mother refuses to see.

programme.title: I Trapped the Devil
programme.desc: Hoping for a joyful family reunion, Matt and his wife pay a surprise visit to the home of his estranged brother, Steve, to celebrate Christmas. To their shock, they soon learn that Steve has a hostage in his basement -- a man he claims is the devil.

programme.title: Fingerprints
programme.desc: A series of murders are linked to the tragic deaths of schoolchildren 50 years earlier.

programme.title: Airborne
programme.desc: A plane takes off during a snowstorm and soon reports both pilots are dead. As the passengers slowly die off, they wish they had never left the ground.

programme.title: The Hallow
programme.desc: A family is forced to fight demonic creatures for survival after they move into a remote mill house in Ireland.

programme.title: Dementia
programme.desc: Hired as a live-in nurse, a seemingly sweet woman (Kristina Klebe) unleashes her dark side as she torments a Vietnam War veteran (Gene Jones) who has dementia.

programme.title: Depraved
programme.desc: A disillusioned field surgeon who is suffering from post-traumatic stress disorder makes a man out of body parts and brings him to life in a Brooklyn loft.

programme.title: ATM
programme.desc: A murderous assailant traps three people (Alice Eve, Josh Peck, Brian Geraghty) in a small kiosk containing an automatic teller machine.

programme.title: Teeth and Blood
programme.desc: Detectives Mike Hung and Sasha Colfax uncover an age-old war between rival vampire covens is about to consume humanity.
When Earth's air becomes unbreathable, a message from the future sends a man on a dangerous mission to an unknown world to save the human race.

programme.title: Backcountry
programme.desc: A couple (Missy Peregrym, Jeff Roop) on a deep-wilderness hike become hopelessly lost within an aggressive black bear's territory.

programme.title: Hellbender
programme.desc: A teen and her mother live simply in a home in the woods, spending their time making metal music. A chance encounter with a fellow teen causes her to uncover a connection between her family and witchcraft, which causes a rift with her mother.

programme.desc: Astronauts (Christian Slater, Brendan Fehr) come under attack by dangerous alien spores after a meteor collides with their moon base.

programme.title: No Man's Land
programme.desc: When a vigilante border patrol turns fatal, a man flees on horseback to Mexico, seeking forgiveness from the victim's father.

programme.desc: A woman inherits a house in her ancestral village, but she is unaware that members of the community have been trying to locate and kill her to remove the curse that has plagued them for years.

programme.title: Exam

programme.desc: Terror strikes when an Orthodox Jewish man agrees to watch over the dead body of a community member in a dilapidated Brooklyn home.

programme.title: The Lost Neanderthals
programme.desc: The Mandrin Cave in southern France has been attracting the attention of archaeologists for 30 years. The cave was regularly occupied in prehistoric times, and a remarkable record of human habitation going back thousands of years has been preserved by its soils. In 2015, the remains of a Neanderthal were uncovered, and the archaeological evidence points to this individual, named Thorin, living in close proximity to newly arrived Homo sapiens. This documentary follows an international team of scientists as they discover and investigate precious relics of the Palaeolithic period, resulting in groundbreaking revelations about the human populations of around 50,000 years ago as researchers uncover some of the secrets of the Mandrin Cave.

programme.title: Timewatch
programme.desc: First transmitted in 2001, this programme chronicles the construction of the Empire State Building in New York, which was the world's tallest skyscraper when opened in 1931. The programme investigates the building's history through interviews with the people who contributed to the construction of this iconic building.

programme.desc: Daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson unlocks the ancient roots of the Northumberland village of Warkworth. With the help of locals, he discovers clues that point back almost 1,000 years to the Norman conquest when the invaders laid the foundations of a planned community, still visible to this day.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: The reality of life afloat with Robbie Cumming. On the last leg of his journey across England, Robbie crosses an epic aqueduct near Stratford-upon-Avon and gets stuck in a lock in central Birmingham.

programme.title: Murder on the Orient Express
programme.desc: Returning to England from Istanbul on the Orient Express, Hercule Poirot is asked to investigate a murder.

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at television appearances made over the years by award-winning actor Albert Finney, with interviews from the archive and classic clips capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.

programme.title: The Importance of Being Earnest
programme.desc: Screen adaptation of Oscar Wilde's celebrated play in which two Victorian gentlemen both adopt a double identity under the name of Ernest and end up in trouble when they both turn up at the same country house one weekend.

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Sun
programme.desc: Ninety-million miles away from us is the power that shapes our world - the sun. We see it shine in the sky above us, but beyond our sight, something dramatic is happening - the sun is going into overdrive. It's more active now than it's been for a decade, sending eruptions of super-heated plasma and vast waves of radiation towards our planet. With the potential to disrupt our lives in dramatic ways. Using the latest satellite images, and the expertise of Britain's leading solar scientists, Kate Humble and Helen Czerski reveal the inner workings of our very own star, and the influence its mysterious cycles of activity have on our planet. They discover why the light reaching us from the sun can be up to a million years old; they meet the teams who protect us by keeping a round-the-clock vigil on the sun; and investigate why some scientists think longer term changes in the sun's behaviour may have powerful effects on our climate.

programme.title: The Riviera: A History in Pictures
programme.desc: Richard E Grant explores how modern art and the Riviera grew up together when France's Cote d'Azur became the hedonistic playground and experimental studio for the great masters of 20th-century painting. With Henri Matisse and Pablo Picasso resident on the coast, other artists from Jean Cocteau to Henri Lartigue, Raoul Dufy to Fernand Leger and Francis Picabia to Sergei Diaghilev were drawn to the area. As transatlantic liners brought America's super-rich to the region, art and celebrity became integrally intertwined as cultural gurus and multimillionaires all partied on the beach. In an era of sunshine and bathing, of cinema and fast cars, of the Ballets Russes and Monte Carlo casinos, Grant discovers the extraordinary output of what became briefly the world's creative hub.

programme.title: The Jet Stream and Us
programme.desc: Documentary tracing how human understanding of the jet stream - a ribbon of fast moving air high in the atmosphere - has grown. It has been responsible for bewildering effect on bomber pilots during the Second World War, turbocharging modern transatlantic flyers, the infamous 1987 hurricane and devastating floods. Scientists believe this powerful weather phenomenon is now changing its pattern of behaviour and could have an even bigger impact on our climate and the way we live our lives. Interviewees include Sir Brian Hoskins, University of Reading and Kirsty McCabe from the BBC Weather Centre.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 24 September 1999 and featuring Vengaboys, Sting, Everything but the Girl, Paul Johnson, Wyclef Jean & Bono, David Bowie, Tom Jones & The Cardigans and Eiffel 65.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jayne Middlemiss presents a roadshow edition of the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 1 October 1999 and featuring DJ Jean, Shania Twain, Kelle Bryan, David Bowie, S Club 7, Britney Spears, Lou Bega and Eiffel 65.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Nicky Campbell present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 28 April 1988 and featuring Pat and Mick, James Brown, Fairground Attraction, Brenda Russell, The Primitives, Luther Vandross, Joyce Sims, Bananarama, Will Downing, S'Express and Scott Fitzgerald.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 25 April 1991 and featuring EMF, Vic Reeves and Electronic.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty is busy this week as the garden bursts into colour. Woody herbs such as thyme and sage don’t thrive in his clay soil, so he’s sowing seeds to ensure a steady supply. He’s planting grasses in the new Woodland Garden and creating a colourful pot for shade, and there's advice on what to do with this year’s daffodil bulbs. Carol Klein proves that small can be both beautiful and delicious, creating a modest border devoted to vegetables and weaving it together with flowers to provide year round colour. And Advolly Richmond heads to Fife to uncover the story of the first pink cupped daffodil, bred in the early 1900s and still growing today thanks to two passionate plantswomen. There’s a visit to a specialist nursery in Somerset. With help from the Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew, they are preparing to exhibit trees fit for a changing climate at the RHS Chelsea Flower show. And it's all aboard a houseboat that's jam-packed with plants.

programme.title: Dusty Springfield at the BBC
programme.desc: A second chance to explore the BBC’s archives with one of British music’s biggest and best-loved stars, Dusty Springfield. The great soul diva and her haunting voice are captured once again in selection of BBC performances from over the years, including You Don’t Have to Say You Love Me, Son of a Preacher Man, and Bacharach and David’s Look of Love. Together, these songs underline why Dusty earned her reputation as one of pop’s great talents, who deserves her ranking alongside fellow singing legends like Aretha Franklin and Dionne Warwick.

programme.title: Dusty Springfield at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of Dusty Springfield's performances at the BBC from 1961 to 1995. Dusty was one of Britain's great pop divas, guaranteed to give us a big melody in songs soaring with drama and yearning. The clips show Dusty's versatility as an artist and performer and include songs from her folk beginnings with The Springfields; the melodrama of You Don't Have to Say You Love Me; Dusty's homage to Motown with Heatwave and Nowhere to Run; the Jacques Brel song If You Go Away; the Bacharach and David tune The Look of Love; and Dusty's collaboration with Pet Shop Boys in the late 1980s. There are also some great duets from Dusty's career with Tom Jones and Mel Torme.

programme.title: Sad Songs at the BBC
programme.desc: A journey through a selection of some of the saddest but finest songs from the BBC’s music archives. Amongst the artists performing are Paul McCartney, Kate Bush, The Smiths, Randy Crawford, Coldplay, Dolly Parton and Eric Clapton, as well as Eric Carmen singing his anthem for the lonely, All By Myself.

programme.title: Dusty Springfield at the BBC
programme.desc: A second chance to explore the BBC’s archives with one of British music’s biggest and best-loved stars, Dusty Springfield. The great soul diva and her haunting voice are captured once again in selection of BBC performances from over the years, including You Don’t Have to Say You Love Me, Son of a Preacher Man, and Bacharach and David’s Look of Love. Together, these songs underline why Dusty earned her reputation as one of pop’s great talents, who deserves her ranking alongside fellow singing legends like Aretha Franklin and Dionne Warwick.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 24 September 1999 and featuring Vengaboys, Sting, Everything but the Girl, Paul Johnson, Wyclef Jean & Bono, David Bowie, Tom Jones & The Cardigans and Eiffel 65.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jayne Middlemiss presents a roadshow edition of the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 1 October 1999 and featuring DJ Jean, Shania Twain, Kelle Bryan, David Bowie, S Club 7, Britney Spears, Lou Bega and Eiffel 65.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Nicky Campbell present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 28 April 1988 and featuring Pat and Mick, James Brown, Fairground Attraction, Brenda Russell, The Primitives, Luther Vandross, Joyce Sims, Bananarama, Will Downing, S'Express and Scott Fitzgerald.

programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.desc: Daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs presents an edition of the old-time music hall programme, filmed in 1973 from the stage of the City Varieties Theatre, Leeds. Guests include Bernard Cribbins, Sandie Shaw, Bill McCue and Frances Van Dyke.

programme.title: Wildlife on Two
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a profile of the pelican, the large water bird noted for its huge pouch and clumsy, waddling gait.

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The victim of a road rage assault enlists the help of Hetty to find out the identity of her assailant.

programme.title: The Count of Monte Cristo
programme.desc: France 1815: Betrayed on his wedding day, young sailor Edmond Dantès is falsely accused of treason and condemned to years of brutal imprisonment in the Château d’If, where an unexpected encounter plants the seed of revenge. Based on the novel by Alexandre Dumas.

programme.title: The Count of Monte Cristo
programme.desc: Escaping prison and uncovering a legendary fortune, Edmond reinvents himself as the mysterious Count of Monte Cristo and returns to a world that has prospered without him. Based on the novel by Alexandre Dumas.

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Sitcom. Hyacinth and Richard go to join the Major at a hotel for a golfing weekend. The Major cries off playing, but has organised a friend to play with Richard.

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Timothy is suffering from bouts of sleepwalking, which could cause trouble as the annual interview for promotion is just around the corner.

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at television appearances made over the years by award-winning actor Albert Finney, with interviews from the archive and classic clips capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Leonard Sachs presents an edition of the old-time music hall programme, filmed in 1973 from the stage of the City Varieties Theatre, Leeds. Guests include Bernard Cribbins, Sandie Shaw, Bill McCue and Frances Van Dyke.

programme.title: AI Decoded
programme.desc: The AI revolution demands vast amounts of electricity. New data centres powering the next generation of artificial intelligence can use as much energy as a small city. Last year, the world's biggest tech firms spent more than 400 billion dollars expanding this infrastructure and demand is still growing. But there's a problem. In the United States, four in ten data centre projects planned for this year face serious delays, and increasingly the public are raising concerns about living next door to these colossal digital factories.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Talking Business
programme.desc: Water has been a flashpoint in the Middle East and worldwide demand is outstripping supply, so how do we make sure there's enough to go round? This most basic resource is vital for drinking, sanitation and agriculture, but also for jobs and economies everywhere. The World Bank's global director for water Saroj Kumar Jha explains why they're trying to make it more of a priority for government spending. And Mareen Schneider, the founder and CEO of project developer Grasshopper Investments, explains how she's coming up with radical new ways for the private sector to invest in water. Plus, Ben Thompson also hears from José Manuel Entrecanales, the CEO of Spanish infrastructure giant Acciona, about the projects they're building around the world and why we need to rethink the value we give to water.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Tech Now
programme.desc: Harriet Bradshaw finds out how AI is being used to help solve an art history mystery. Andrew Rogers heads behind the scenes of Pinewood Studios to see how the latest advances in technology are taking filmmaking to new heights. Suranjana Tewari explores the new innovations at the Beijing Autoshow. And Laura Cress chats to games designer Peter Molyneux about his final release, Masters of Albion.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Clive Myrie’s Caribbean Adventure
programme.desc: The Platinum Coast of Barbados is known for its golden sands and wealthy inhabitants. Clive is more interested in why this tiny country produces so many international cricketers and meets one of the island’s top coaches for a few tips on his batting. He also joins in with a local tradition of a morning swim with Barbados’s top racehorses and trainers, before jumping on a boat to land an enormous Caribbean catch for a classic Bajan cookout.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: On the Front Line
programme.desc: It started with the seizure of illegal cigarettes hidden in the back room of a minimart in Rochdale and ended with cocaine being bought over the counter from a shop in the West Midlands. Over a year, BBC news investigated the crime happening in plain sight on high streets across the country and, in the process, exposed the crime gangs exploiting the vulnerable and setting up shop in Britain.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Media Show
programme.desc: A dispute around the latest series of I’m a Celebrity… Get Me Out of Here has drawn attention to how reality television is edited. Some contestants have said that footage was cut in a misleading way, affecting how they were shown on screen. The broadcaster ITV has said the programme reflects events fairly and accurately. Lauren Morris, culture news editor at The Independent, alongside reality television producers Donald Clarke and Rob Davis discuss the processes and standards used in production. King Charles’s state visit to the US has taken place at a time of diplomatic tension, following criticism from President Trump over the UK’s position on the war with Iran. The visit, the first by a British monarch in nearly two decades, involved a series of carefully planned public events and took place under heightened security. Sean Coughlan, royal correspondent at BBC News, and Jack Blanchard, author of POLITICO’s Playbook newsletter, outline their reporting approaches and editorial context.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Travel Show
programme.desc: Adventurer and UK Chief Scout Dwayne Fields embarks on a journey through Zambia. In the past decade, the African nation has become one of the fastest emerging tourist destinations in the world. And to kick off, Dwayne heads to its most recognisable and most visited attraction, Victoria Falls. He explores the foaming rapids at the base of the waterfall, before heading to the country's lively capital, Lusaka, to take part in a traditional pre-wedding ceremony. He ends his adventure in the remote interior, where he witnesses the world's largest mammal migration - the arrival of ten million fruit bats at Kasanka National Park.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: In a special scams episode, the team meets the 20-year-old who was waiting to receive a tax rebate from HMRC, only to discover someone else had cashed the cheque weeks earlier. A fraud lawyer is on hand to explain what went wrong. Also on the programme, the advice clinic visits Reading to hear from one man who used social media to complain about missing Christmas presents he had ordered – and was unknowingly targeted by scammers. And the latest how-to guide reveals exactly what to do if your phone gets stolen.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion is in Derby to see a plot of land, with a whole family getting involved to build a brand new house. A separate family project in Lowestoft, Suffolk, sees an auntie and nephew tackle a tired terrace. And in another Suffolk property, Jacqui meets a novice renovator more used to dealing with cuts of meat than knocking down walls.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Since the Hollywood move was released, vintage Barbie dolls have shot up in value. Natasha Raskin Sharp meets collector Dawn Austin, whose collection of more than 800 dolls takes up an entire room of her house! Natasha learns that some vintage models are worth thousands, but does a first edition Ken doll command the same price as an early Barbie? In the Derbyshire town of Heanor, experts Mark Stacey and Catherine Southon lead the reds and blues around a huge antiques centre. Mark guides couple Terry and Alison, who plan to ‘spend little and win big’, while Catherine’s team, Craig and Dave, are looking for ‘things that are big, bold and beautiful’. During their shop, Dave spots a bingo ball wheel – but would they hit the jackpot if they took it to auction? Meanwhile, Terry has his eye on a giant eagle, but Mark’s not sure the carved bird will fly with the bidders at Colin Young’s saleroom in Grantham.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: In Eastbourne, siblings David and Theresa battle to raise money for DKMS, gambling on designer fashion, vintage finds and bold upcycled pieces.

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: Banjo and his team are on South Uist in the Outer Hebrides. They’ve been called in to help crofters DJ and Lindsay, whose family have lived and worked the land there for generations. The couple want to turn their garage into a shop to sell their produce from. The dull breezeblock building has no character or charm, so Banjo has to work from scratch to transform this empty space. He searches for salvage around the island and from DJ’s crofter mum, and he decides to clad the walls in disused corrugated iron panels he finds around the island. But with a budget of only £4,000, will he pull off his homestead kitchen design vision?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A young couple want to swap their London flat for something bigger in the East Midlands, where Nicki Chapman opens the front doors to a lovely selection of homes with charming gardens. Nicki can’t miss the opportunity to meet another young couple who’ve already made their escape to develop a luxury leather goods business. After all, Nicki does love a good bag!

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Pyrographer Chantal from Leeds and stained-glass artist Claire from Norfolk both hope to make their dreams of a creative career come true. Having spent several years trying to earn a living from pyrography, Chantal feels it’s her last chance to make her craft pay. She has a thyroid condition which causes extreme fatigue, but she’s prepared to do whatever it takes to make her business dream a reality. After having to give up her sugar craft shop following a 33-year career, Claire needs to find a new way of earning a living and has her sights set on a new stained-glass business to pay the bills. Mentor Derek thinks she has the talent and potential to do well, but can Claire learn the new skills needed to succeed? Both are given two months to take on board all the advice they’re given and turn their passions into paying professions.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the quiz show where a team of four – in this episode, family members Conn, Paula, Brian and Sophia – take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie and the Bridge will halve their money. Step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Angela Scanlon for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Can you trust airport departure boards? The team meet one group of passengers who waited patiently in the departure lounge for their delayed flight, only to find out it had already left without them. Also on the programme, an investigation into one of the biggest names in travel finds it’s not following the rules designed to protect people whose package holidays go wrong. And the advice clinic team head to Mansfield to help one woman who has spent more than £10,000 trying to exit her timeshare agreement and is now fielding calls from scammers.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A tale of two very different million-pound-plus projects, one in central London, the other in Anglesey. Martin’s also in Bristol to see a two-bedroom flat.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: There’s no time for horsing around as two teams canter through an antiques fair at Wetherby racecourse in West Yorkshire. In red, and saddling up for some fun, are sisters Sharon and Hayley, with their expert Charles Hanson. On the blue team, brothers Harley and Joe are paired with expert Philip Serrell. With one hour on the clock, the reds close in on an item that’s on their shopping list – an enamelled scent bottle – but they turn their noses up when they learn the best price. Over with the blues, Phil tries to spice things up with a silver nutmeg grater. Sadly, the three-figure price tag puts them off, so they stop to consider another important question – which sauce they prefer on their chips! Meanwhile, Danny Sebastian is dishing up a challenge for fairgoers, asking them to identify a mystery metal object. It’s silver-plated with a plunger at one end and a perforated acorn-shaped bowl at the other. What could it be?

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Rock 'n' roll reaches Kembleford when a group of bikers cause outrage and excitement in equal measure.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown is on hand to help a couple of newlyweds looking to escape South London and set down roots in the stunning countryside of Cumbria. They’ve already discounted a dozen properties, so the pressure is on for Steve to deliver somewhere that will become their very first home together.

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Blacksmith Clare from Solihull and baker Gavin from Bedfordshire both dream of building businesses from their passions. After deciding teaching wasn’t for her, Clare turned to metalwork and retrained as a blacksmith. Having just finished her studies, she needs to start making money so that she and her partner can save up a deposit for their first home together. Orchestra box office manager Gavin hopes that baking will also help him save enough money to move house. He currently lives with his mum, and although she is the inspiration behind his baking, he wants his own place with more space for him and his equipment. Both have talent, and their mentors believe they could be booking the removal vans in no time, but will Clare and Gavin put in the hard graft to be successful?

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents a celebrity edition of the quiz show, where a team of soap stars, featuring Coronation Street’s Faye Brookes, Hollyoaks’s Richard Blackwood, Natalie Anderson from Emmerdale and EastEnders’ John Partridge, get together to take on the Bridge of Lies for their chosen charities. One by one they all face the bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each bridge crossing has 32 potential steps - 22 truths and 10 lies - and they must cross by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Once they have each faced the bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash.

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.desc: A rare medical condition meant Grace was unable to give birth, as she was born without a womb. But in a landmark moment for British medical history, her sister Amy made an extraordinary decision: to donate her womb to her sister in a complicated and involved operation, which lasted hours. Just months after the transplant, Grace became pregnant and now, with her husband, has a healthy daughter. It was a gift that transformed not only the sisters’ lives but those of everyone around them in a story of love, sacrifice and a deep family bond. Bill Bailey pairs Grace and Amy with Karen Turner, an award-winning artist with a powerful commitment to telling women’s stories. Karen works using an unusual and unforgiving technique, painting directly onto raw canvas. With no background layer and no possibility of correction, every paint stroke must be right. As Karen begins her work, she faces a profound challenge: how to capture the depth of love, trust and sacrifice that made this miracle possible. Can she find a way to express not just the medical achievement but the emotional truth behind it and create a portrait worthy of this remarkable act of sisterhood?

programme.title: Beyond Paradise
programme.desc: It’s Humphrey’s last day as detective inspector, but there’s no time to dwell, as the whole team is eagerly awaiting the town’s annual pirate festival. Danger, however, is ahoy when Geraldine Desai’s nautical exhibition goes up in smoke, leading to the ancient treasure map of pirate Elias Cutler going missing amid the chaos. On the trail of the missing map, Humphrey soon has suspicions when he discovers that Geraldine has been selling her memorabilia to pay for her husband’s care and recently had the map revalued. Touching base with an antiques dealer, who is adamant the map is worth nothing, the team begin to question if they’re dealing with a harmless prank, an insurance job, or something more sinister. Meanwhile, Humphrey frets about a potential leaving party as Mr Smith prepares to take over. Smelling a rat, Anne recruits Martha and Kelby to launch an investigation of her own. Elsewhere, Esther braces herself to say goodbye to her daughter, unaware that Zoe has prepared a shock surprise.

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade hosts, with guest panellists actor and comedian Stephen Mangan and journalist Ava Santina Evans joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the news.

programme.desc: Mairéad and the lads take the family car on a road trip to pick up her unpredictable mother, but disaster strikes.

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: Asia has the longest coastline of any continent and the richest coral reefs on earth. Its seas are connected by some of the world’s most powerful currents and are home to over 60 per cent of all marine species. This groundbreaking film features the charismatic creatures of Asia's oceans, never-seen-before spectacles and brand new animal behaviour. From ocean giants in the big blue to tiny fish that climb trees, squid that glow in the dark and grey reef sharks in their hundreds, Asia’s waters are as spectacular as they are diverse.

programme.title: Freddie Flintoff's Field of Dreams
programme.desc: Sporting hero Freddie Flintoff returns to Preston with plans to take his team of unlikely lads on a cricket tour to India. But weeks before departure, a life-changing accident threatens to put a halt to their dreams. One year later, Freddie emerges back to the safety net of his team to announce the trip is back on, but once in India, the team must learn to live with each other and fend for themselves as they prepare to compete in one of the most revered cricketing nations in the world.

programme.title: The Traitors India
programme.desc: Twenty players enter a grand palace to play the ultimate game of trust and betrayal, hosted by Karan Johar. As they vie for the ten million rupee prize, an early twist sets alliances and rivalries into motion. The Traitors begin their silent sabotage, hiding in plain sight. In the first mission, the players solve riddles aboard a moving train to avert disaster, with their teamwork and quick thinking put to the ultimate test. As night falls, the Traitors step out of the shadows to plot their first move. Clueless of what the night holds, Innocents await the first judgement of their fate in this deadly game.

programme.title: The Traitors India
programme.desc: Tensions rise as the players grapple with the aftermath of a shocking murder. The air is heavy with mistrust as the group prepares for the day’s mission - a test of their ability to work together despite growing fractures. In a challenge that pushes them to think like their fellow players, some of them volunteer to spin on a giant wheel, while others try to match answers with them to build the prize pot. At the Circle of Shaq, the Innocents get their first opportunity to strike back and expose the Traitors. Accusations run wild and alliances shift as the group struggles to separate truth from deception. Will the Innocents succeed in identifying the Traitors, or will their web of lies hold strong?

programme.title: The Traitors India
programme.desc: After tasting success in their first attempt, the Innocents feel a renewed sense of hope, but their joy is fleeting as the Traitors ruthlessly claim their next victim in the dead of the night. The players are divided into two groups for a critical mission: collecting gold nuggets by balancing on slippery planks over a water body. The team with the most gold wins a shield, granting one player protection from the next murder, but the mission exposes cracks in alliances as players crack under pressure. After the mission, Karan leaves the players with a shocking twist - a blast from the past - forcing the group to re-evaluate everything they thought they knew.

programme.title: Better Date Than Never
programme.desc: Charles is off to a bumpy start, and Olivia is left speechless after meeting her latest match. Meanwhile, Nirvali debriefs with her family, and Dianne gets a pep talk from her mum.

programme.title: Better Date Than Never
programme.desc: A phone call sets Olivia’s heart racing, Charles gets back on the horse, and Nirvali opens up. Meanwhile, 20-year-old Liv already has her wedding planned but needs to find her groom.

programme.title: Matthew Perry and the Ketamine Queen
programme.desc: Documentary investigating events leading up to the shocking death of Friends star Matthew Perry and the woman at the centre of the case, dubbed the 'Ketamine Queen' by US prosecutors. Jasveen Sangha, a drug dealer and dual US-UK citizen, has pleaded guilty to five federal charges in Los Angeles, including one count of distributing ketamine resulting in death or bodily injury. She faces up to 65 years in prison for supplying Perry with the drugs that led to his death. Sangha was one of five people charged in the case. Prosecutors described her home as a 'drug-selling emporium', where authorities found approximately 79 vials of ketamine and other drugs, including methamphetamine, cocaine and Xanax. Presented by Amber Haque, the film features exclusive, first-on-the-record interviews with Sangha's friends and examines her role in Perry’s death and Hollywood’s ketamine drug network.

programme.title: Fake Friend: The Ticket Scammer
programme.desc: How a Somerset high-flyer turned his charm and connections into tools of deception – conning his school friends and hundreds of others while living a life of glamour himself.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Brothers Steve and Stu scramble to put on a last-minute birthday show for Steve’s daughter. If they fail, Steve’s in the doghouse and Stu might end up... dead.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Animated sitcom set in 1990s east London, following the lives of the chaotic Potts family. Joyce Potts’s birthday present has been stolen, so her husband Big Jim enlists the help of his fearsome mother-in-law Edna to track it down on the Thames View Estate.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve heads to Zambia for the first time. He hears hippo communication on a whole new level, spots a high-diving croc and meets some incredible sniffer dogs.

programme.title: Despicable Me 2
programme.desc: Gru is recruited by the Anti-Villain League to go undercover to track down an unknown supervillain who has stolen an entire secret research laboratory. He is given an assistant, Lucy, who has some tricks up her sleeve.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Marlon receives a mysterious phone call from his old boss, who sounds badly injured as he begs him for help. It’s a blast from his criminal past, and someone he’s not spoken to since joining the police force. When Marlon hurries over, he’s horrified to find his former boss dead – having been shot. And the only witness is a parrot... Marlon’s determined to solve the case, insistent that – although his former boss dabbled in criminality – he wasn’t a bad guy. But when the team uncover two potential suspects, they’re frustrated to find themselves at a stalemate. Whilst one suspect has every opportunity but absolutely no motive, the other has every motive but a rock-solid alibi.

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: Janie is tasked with solving the murder of divisive best man Gethin Bennion the night before a wedding. What initially seems to be a burglary gone wrong quickly unravels complicated family dynamics and betrayed friendships, revealing just how far people will go for love. Janie is forced to face up to her personal experience of a friendship turned sour in order to connect with a suspect and crack the case. Meanwhile, John forms an unlikely connection with one of the suspects over art and painting, forcing him to re-evaluate the role that work played in his own marriage and the circumstances around his late wife’s passing.

programme.title: Death Valley
programme.desc: Following the murder of coastal town businesswoman and am-dram theatre director Menna Hughes, Janie is eager to solve the case and land the DI promotion she desperately wants. She enlists John’s help, but bristling at Janie’s insinuation that he might have ‘lost’ his acting chops, John takes matters into his own hands and joins the cast of the theatre group’s next play. What starts out as a murder investigation soon becomes a cathartic journey for John, who is unexpectedly reminded of why he fell in love with acting in the first place. As for Janie, witnessing a downtrodden suspect finally stand up for themselves inspires her to confront her own demons from the past.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: All aboard the Teesside Princess for Mam’s Titanic-themed drag brunch, but the event hits a proverbial iceberg when Mam is faced with an unwelcome blast from her past.

programme.title: Smoggie Queens
programme.desc: It’s games night, and Dickie has concocted 'an absolutely amazing' murder mystery evening full of twists and turns. Solving the murder should be easy, if he can keep it all on track.

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: Dr David Graham is online and looking for love. He seems to have it all: the looks, the job, and the charisma. On paper, he’s Kilmarnock’s most eligible bachelor. But when the town’s former beauty queen Abbie Draper starts to receive messages from him after he treats her grandfather in hospital, she gets the feeling he might be too good to be true. She turns online sleuth and starts to uncover a trail of deception, crazy coincidences and stories that just don’t add up. Dr David isn’t just a bad boyfriend; something more sinister is afoot. Abbie starts trying to figure out who David Graham really is and why he is doing this.

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: Dr David’s victims begin comparing their stories, and it becomes clear they are dealing with a sophisticated catfish. But nothing could prepare them for the shocking truth of the perpetrator’s true identity, which leaves the women with more questions than answers. But when beauty queen Abbie takes these findings to the police, they are unable to do anything about it, and the Catfish of Kilmarnock remains free to target more and more victims. Abbie can’t walk away, and steps up her investigation over months that turn into years. As she continues to track the catfish, the scale of their criminality comes into focus, with victims spread across the country and dating back over a decade. This is one of the UK’s most prolific ever catfish, and they are not done yet.

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: As criminal investigations take hold, the victims finally have justice, but they are still left urgently searching for answers as they try to rebuild their lives. What drives someone, with a seemingly normal life, to throw it all away to terrorise countless women from the same town? With no clear motive and no explanation from the person in the frame, this very modern true crime tale now turns to those closest to the perpetrator, who are also trying to understand the mystery behind the repeated cycles of offending. As the beauty queen and the catfish’s lives become ever more entangled, lines begin to blur, loyalties are tested, and Abbie finds herself questioning everything as history repeats, and the moral complexity of crime and punishment in the internet age is laid bare. For the many victims, the question remains: what drives someone to become one of the UK’s most prolific catfish - and why can’t they stop?

programme.desc: Four very different schoolgirls are thrown together in detention. As they plan their escape, the situation spirals out of control.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: When the Doctor arrives in Victorian London he finds a dinosaur rampant in the Thames and a spate of deadly spontaneous combustions. Who is the new Doctor and will Clara's friendship with him survive as they embark on a terrifying mission into the heart of an alien conspiracy? The Doctor has changed. It's time you knew him.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: When a historical documentary team comes to film at Button House, Alison and the ghosts are shocked to discover that the assassination plot they are investigating involved one of their own. Meanwhile, Mike - always on the lookout for free publicity - begs Alison to accept the offer of a television interview about the old house. The only hitch is that Alison is terrified of speaking on camera. Luckily, Thomas is there to coach her in the art of speechmaking. What could possibly go wrong?

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A mysterious visitor arrives at Button House with a revelation that leaves Alison reeling. Thomas is equally confused by his feelings for this strange visitor, while Lady Button and Julian warn of sinister motives, and Kitty is as territorial as ever over her surrogate sister Alison. Mike has an epiphany on his first day at a new job, which triggers Julian to remember his first day as a ghost. But will their insights be enough to help Alison make sense of the overwhelming news she has received?

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda isn't your average girly girl. At 6ft 1in, Miranda gets called 'sir' once too often, much to the amusement of her childhood friend Stevie, who manages Miranda's joke shop. Miranda's mother Penny is disappointed that Miranda runs a joke shop rather than having a proper job. Penny bumps into one of Miranda's old school friends, Tilly, and lies that Miranda has a great new job, so Miranda decides to prove everyone wrong and find a new career. After getting caught by Tilly while helping out as a waitress, she decides to come clean and be proud of her real job, but, thanks to Gary, Tilly jumps to a surprising conclusion about Miranda's employment.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda knows she is not exactly cool, but being young, free and single has its advantages, like jetting off to Thailand on holiday for a few days. Although when it comes down to it, she would rather not bother with the hassle of going quite so far, so she books herself into a hotel across the road instead. She thoroughly enjoys the hotel, and even makes new friends with helpful porter Jason and conference organiser Colin. As her stay goes on, she literally becomes a new woman.

programme.title: Quiz Lady
programme.desc: A game show-obsessed young woman and her estranged train wreck of a sister must work together to help cover their mother’s gambling debts. Together, they set out on a wild, cross-country trek to get the cash the only way they know – by becoming bona fide game show champions.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet and Donna compete for a job managing the pub. Louise buys a car with her mother's legacy. Jonny realises that Gaz is the father of Corinthian and gives him to Gaz, until Gaz reveals that Corinthian can't be his due to his low sperm count.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet starts her job as manager of The Archer and gives the pub a World War Two theme to appeal to the grey pound. Donna is intent on revenge, Jonny tries to help Gaz become a dad, and Louise becomes a prostitute.

programme.title: Beyond Paradise
programme.desc: An off-grid community is thrust into the spotlight when a land developer is terrorised by the image of a Green Man. As tensions rise, secrets surface that someone wants buried.

programme.title: Pilgrimage
programme.desc: The pilgrims are in the Scottish Borders on St Cuthbert’s Way in the Cheviots. It takes them on a strenuous route up Wideopen Hill to reach some spectacular views. Later, the pilgrims head to St Cuthbert’s Cave in the Kyloe Hills, where legend says the monks rested with Cuthbert’s body when they fled Lindisfarne in the late 9th century in the wake of Viking raids. And on the last day of their pilgrimage, they follow the 5km walk across the tidal sands of Pilgrims' Way, where saints and pilgrims have trekked for over a thousand years. Finally, they arrive at Holy Island, where they reflect on a journey that has brought some surprising emotional revelations.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: After viewing more than 200 houses, a couple from the city of Sheffield is hopeful that Cornwall will finally deliver their dream countryside home, with enough space for their extensive book and film memorabilia. Briony May Williams takes on the challenge, lining up some inspiring properties, including a mystery house that quickly becomes one of her all-time favourites. Briony rounds off her visit with a refreshing dip in Bude’s famous sea swimming pool.

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: In Harrogate, former Traitors stars Sonja and Aubrey swap strategy for style, hunting vintage tweed and bold 80s fashion to raise money for a cat sanctuary.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Boarding the superfast Shinkansen bullet train, Michael heads north from Tokyo to Honshu’s Tohoku region, known as the rice granary of Japan. He visits a fifth-generation rice farm to discover how the crop is cultivated and processed, and takes a turn around the field on a combine harvester. Continuing his route north, Michael’s next stop is the city of Sendai. He learns how disaster struck in March 2011, when the coast was hit by a powerful earthquake and a devastating tsunami. On the roof of a primary school in the neighbourhood of Arahama, which overlooks the beach, the former principal shares his powerful memories of the fateful day and his plan to save his pupils. Further up the coast at the fishing port of Shiogama, Michael attends the daily tuna auction and watches as bidders compete for the prized fish, with the largest fetching almost £1,900. The town boasts more sushi restaurants per capita than anywhere in the country, and Michael enjoys a tasting with a master chef.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As the garden explodes with spring colour, Monty adds a dash of Mediterranean flair and flavour by planting lemons in pots. He also plants in the woodland garden and pots up summer bulbs. Sue Kent is in her vibrant Swansea garden, sowing tender crops, planting echiums and harvesting a delicious salad bowl. Jamie Butterworth is in Essex to discover why the humble euphorbia deserves to be considered one of the brightest stars of the border. There’s also a trip to West Sussex to visit renowned garden designer Rosemary Alexander in her private garden. Plus a passionate anemone grower preparing for their debut at the RHS Chelsea Flower Show.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 13 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of classic recipes from some of the BBC's best-loved television chefs. This episode features dishes that are great to turn to when time is tight - and it's speed, not vanilla, that is of the essence. The menu includes Nigella Lawson's sausage meatballs, Nigel Slater's herb butter squid, James Martin's icebox cake, the Hairy Bikers' Welsh rarebit and Mary Berry's rich chocolate tray bake with feathered icing.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Deep in the rainforest of Central Africa lies an elephant oasis - a remarkable place that holds the key to the future for forest elephants. Over the last 20 years, Andrea Turkalo has been studying these enigmatic giants, getting to know over 4,000 intimately. She has begun to unravel the secrets of their complex social lives and the meanings of their unique vocalisations. New acoustic research is shedding light on the many mysteries that still surround forest elephant society. Will these endangered elephants finally speak out and tell Andrea what it is they need to survive?

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Today, Flog It! comes from Dover Castle in Kent. Antique experts Catherine Southon and Jonathan Pratt search for items to take off to auction. Catherine falls for a colourful brooch and Jonathan unearths a horn beaker used by Winston Churchill. Paul Martin uncovers the story of Dover Castle’s secret tunnels.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Kelle Bryan, Antony Costa, Alexei Sayle and Emma Sidi take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games wheelie luggage, or will they be tempted with the very popular bee hotel?

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael’s bullet train takes him through the undersea Seikan tunnel to Hokkaido, the most northerly of Japan’s main islands and home to the indigenous Ainu people. He begins by exploring the southern city of Hakodate, one of the ports which was first to trade with the outside world. Visiting a family-run miso factory, he finds out how the staple Japanese ingredient is made using soybeans and a cultured mould known as koji. In the centre of the city, Michael visits the star-shaped Fort Goryokaku, the setting for a turning point in Japanese history. He discovers how the last remnants of the Tokugawa military forces were defeated in 1869 by the new Emperor Meiji’s army, ushering in a new modern era for Japan. Swapping the Shinkansen for a slower pace in a diesel train, Michael leaves the city to explore Hokkaido’s stunning volcanic landscape, travelling to Lake Toya in the Shikotsu-Toya National Park.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 13 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob travel with their trusty terrier Ted to fish the mighty River Trent in Nottingham in search of barbel. In the large, wide river, they can sense the presence of big barbel, but will they land one? Later, they stay in a memorable party house with a bar, games room, nightclub and hot tubs. The boys can really relive their youths at this special place, but will they have the energy to stay up all night? There is a big surprise guest hiding at the venue, and Paul is almost speechless as he meets him, much to Bob’s delight - legendary DJ Tony Blackburn.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Paddy O'Connell.

programme.title: On the Basis of Sex
programme.desc: The story of a young Ruth Bader Ginsburg – then a struggling attorney and new mother – who faces adversity and numerous obstacles in her fight for equal rights throughout her career. When Ruth takes on a groundbreaking tax case with her husband, attorney Martin Ginsburg, she knows it could change the direction of her career and the way the courts view gender discrimination. The film also chronicles the romance between Ruth and Marty, a partnership that succeeded both personally and professionally.

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Six more amateurs work to secure a place in this week’s quarter-final as they are put through their paces by Grace and Anna in the second heat of the week.

programme.title: Michael Jackson: An American Tragedy
programme.desc: Following Jackson from the shockwaves of the Martin Bashir documentary Living with Michael Jackson to the final act of his life - and the battle over what he leaves behind. In the aftermath of the documentary, insiders describe a man in free-fall: frightened, financially exposed and increasingly isolated. Manager Dieter Wiesner recalls Michael rallying his fanbase, while rumours of mounting debts and allegations escalate. When 12-year-old Gavin Arvizo goes to police, prosecutor Ron Zonen asserts Jackson knew it was 'an inappropriate relationship'. The singer is arrested and charged, and becomes the centre of a trial that consumes a small town and the global media.

programme.title: Artemis: To the Moon and Back
programme.desc: The inside story of the Artemis II mission - filmed over three and a half years at NASA. How did four astronauts travel to the moon, going deeper into space than ever before?

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: When tiny dragons, octopuses, sharks and other sea creatures began washing up on the Cornish coast, it started a real-life treasure hunt. Nearly 30 years later, Christina Trevanion remembers the Lego spill of February 1997, when almost five million pieces were washed overboard from a container ship, ending up on beaches across the south west. She is joined by Kate Clayton from the Royal Cornwall Museum, who shares some of the rarest items from their collection. Hoping to piece together a win in Devon are two teams of antiques hunters. On the red team are longtime friends Annie and Sandie, guided by their expert Ben Cooper. Their competition in blue are dad and daughter Ed and Rose, who have Gary Pe by their side. Annie is sweet on a cake plate decorated with hops, but Ben doesn’t see a profit in it, so they move on. Across the antiques centre, Ed thinks a pair of temple lions are 'roarsome', until Gary spots the four-figure price tag. In Chippenham, auctioneer Tim Weeks gives his estimates on the teams’ items before selling them to the highest bidders.

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: In Abergavenny, couple Claudia and Luke clash over instincts and risk everything in a rain-soaked race, ending in a last-minute upcycle scramble and a tense live auction.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael’s bullet train takes him through the undersea Seikan tunnel to Hokkaido, the most northerly of Japan’s main islands and home to the indigenous Ainu people. He begins by exploring the southern city of Hakodate, one of the ports which was first to trade with the outside world. Visiting a family-run miso factory, he finds out how the staple Japanese ingredient is made using soybeans and a cultured mould known as koji. In the centre of the city, Michael visits the star-shaped Fort Goryokaku, the setting for a turning point in Japanese history. He discovers how the last remnants of the Tokugawa military forces were defeated in 1869 by the new Emperor Meiji’s army, ushering in a new modern era for Japan. Swapping the Shinkansen for a slower pace in a diesel train, Michael leaves the city to explore Hokkaido’s stunning volcanic landscape, travelling to Lake Toya in the Shikotsu-Toya National Park.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: Ever wonder if doom-scrolling is draining your joy? You’re not alone. The science says cutting smartphone use to just two hours a day could seriously boost your wellbeing – but actually doing it is another story. So Zoe Ball is in Kettering to share with busy beautician Lucie some clever digital detox tricks that could improve family life, reduce stress and even boost her brain power. Less scrolling, more living.

programme.title: Ronan Keating's Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Ronan Keating continues his epic journey along Ireland’s Wild Atlantic coast. He makes an emotional trip across the waves with his nephew Conall to Clare Island, a special place for the entire Keating family. They get to grips with a traditional loom, before Ronan heads for Sligo to meet up with a fellow boyband veteran, Shane Filan of Westlife, for some horse riding on the golden sands of Strandhill Beach.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 14 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Montell Douglas presents an appeal on behalf of Action for ME, a charity that provides support and healthcare for people of all ages affected by ME, while looking to create positive change for their future through medical research.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary looking at the wildlife of the most stunning mountain range in the world, home to snow leopards, Himalayan wolves and Tibetan bears. Snow leopards stalk their prey among the highest peaks. Concealed by snowfall, the chase is watched by golden eagles circling above. On the harsh plains of the Tibetan plateau live extraordinary bears and square-faced foxes hunting small rodents to survive. In the alpine forests, dancing pheasants have even influenced rival border guards in their ritualistic displays. Valleys carved by glacial waters lead to hillsides covered by paddy fields containing the lifeline to the east, rice. In this world of extremes, the Himalayas reveal not only snow-capped mountains and fascinating animals but also a vital lifeline for humanity.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be alright!

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 14 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Kelle Bryan, Antony Costa, Alexei Sayle and Emma Sidi take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games wheelie luggage, or will they be tempted with the very popular bee hotel?

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the final leg of his Japanese adventure, Michael explores the history of Hokkaido’s indigenous Ainu people. Travelling east to the Iburi region, an area closely associated with Ainu culture, he visits Lake Shikotsu. Over a traditional meal of salmon soup on the lake shore, he learns about the beliefs and values of the Ainu and how they were forced to assimilate to Japanese society following Hokkaido’s colonisation. After lunch, he is treated to a performance of the ancient Dance of the Cranes. Continuing north to Hokkaido’s capital, Sapporo, Michael enjoys the last evening of his journey in the Susukino district, a Japanese nightlife hotspot. Just along the coast from the port of Otaru on the Sea of Japan, Michael ends his tour with a trip to a famous distillery in the small town of Yoichi.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 14 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: The Bafta-nominated Live at the Apollo returns to BBC Two for a highly anticipated 14th series with seven episodes, including a Christmas special. At the forefront of its genre, the roll call of stand-ups who have performed in front of the famous Live at the Apollo lights plays out like a who's who of comedic royalty, and this series is no different. Each episode sees a national (and sometimes international) stand-up compering and performing, before introducing two of the best established and up and coming stand-ups to the stage. This sixth episode is hosted by the critically-acclaimed Jamali Maddix, as he introduces the impressive comedy talents of rising stars Fern Brady and Sindhu Vee to the stage. Other acts to appear throughout the series include Ellie Taylor, Fin Taylor, Tez Ilyas, Sarah Millican, Gary Delaney, Laura Lexx, Ahir Shah, Phil Wang, Rachel Parris, Brennan Reece, Gina Yashere, Lost Voice Guy, Ed Gamble, Kerry Godliman, Suzi Ruffell, Loyiso Gola, Dara O Briain, Felicity Ward and Mo Gilligan.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Matt Chorley.

programme.title: Get Carter
programme.desc: Slick criminal Jack Carter returns to northern England to investigate his brother’s death, causing friction with local villains and some 'respectable' citizens while uncovering a conspiracy.

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Shock waves from the Iran War have already been felt in the UK. Fuel prices have risen and interest rates are on hold, but that could be just the beginning of a much bigger economic impact on energy bills, the price of food and mortgage rates. Reporter Rahil Sheikh meets families already feeling the squeeze, businesses having to adapt to a world turned upside down, and experts trying to make sense of it all. What could the war mean for the economy and the cost of living here at home?

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: It's the first quarter-final, where this week’s six most talented amateurs gather to face off against each other for the first time.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: Alan Carr kicks off the competition by tasking ten interior designers to show off their signature styles by transforming a beach hut on the Dorset coast. They must also demonstrate their ability to reuse and repurpose where they can. Michelle Ogundehin and guest judge Jonathan Adler must decide who has created the Stand Out Space and which designer will be the first to go home.

programme.title: To Think Like a Killer
programme.desc: When the FBI calls, Dr Ann Burgess joins a new task force hunting serial predators and is thrust into an active investigation involving the assault of over 20 women.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: The Piris plan a trip to Tippety Top to see the famous southern lights but lose each other in an ice fog. When Nugget thinks she sees a monster, Pancake proves to be an unlikely hero.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Big Lizard has found an epic crystal, and Cosima is jealous. Their squabbling reaches its peak at the top of the volcano! Will they learn to share before it's too late?

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: The freight train is here! Weasy wants to go shopping, but the ducklings want to play with their teddies. They secretly play while Weasy shops. Just when the train is about to leave, the ducklings realise that their teddy bears are still inside!

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The heroes set out on a quest to find out who is draining colour from the forest and muster all their positivity to save it from greyness.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings turn into crab rescuers, but soon the house is full of them!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck gets a job in a cinema projection room, Frog manages to accidentally break the projector. Can Frog save the day and entertain the audience himself?

programme.title: Pokémon Horizons
programme.desc: After a year apart, Roy visits Liko at Indigo Academy to ask for her help.

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Callum sets up the ultimate Grand Prix, and real F1 drivers give Joe advice on how to win - but the vehicles used are far from being cars! And the bros embark on a Mission Eggpossible.

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.desc: Nitro and Electro prank Fury at a fake wellness retreat! Prepare for fart whistles, bubble yoga, a bogus snail oracle and the slowest game of Unleash ever!

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Steve Backshall joins for a deadly quiz, His Majesty the King is awarded a Blue Peter Green Badge, Abby discovers some deadly flowers and fungi, and it’s the latest read from the Blue Peter Book Club.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Topical news magazine for children.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads out to find one of the UK’s greatest predators: the dragonfly. He witnesses the aerial prowess of the hobby and dives underwater with the puffin.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty is busy this week as the garden bursts into colour. Woody herbs such as thyme and sage don’t thrive in his clay soil, so he’s sowing seeds to ensure a steady supply. He’s planting grasses in the new Woodland Garden and creating a colourful pot for shade, and there's advice on what to do with this year’s daffodil bulbs. Carol Klein proves that small can be both beautiful and delicious, creating a modest border devoted to vegetables and weaving it together with flowers to provide year round colour. And Advolly Richmond heads to Fife to uncover the story of the first pink cupped daffodil, bred in the early 1900s and still growing today thanks to two passionate plantswomen. There’s a visit to a specialist nursery in Somerset. With help from the Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew, they are preparing to exhibit trees fit for a changing climate at the RHS Chelsea Flower show. And it's all aboard a houseboat that's jam-packed with plants.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 15 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of classic recipes from some of the BBC's best-loved television chefs. Ainsley looks at homemade meals that you normally turn to when you are craving a takeaway. The menu includes fish and chips from James Martin, a pizza recipe from Keith Floyd, a curry from Lorraine Pascale, Nigel Slater's Chinese chicken thighs and the Hairy Bikers' take on a pork chow mein.

programme.title: UEFA Women's Champions League
programme.desc: Live coverage as Lyon take on Arsenal in the Women's Champions League.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Flog It! comes from the heart of Birmingham and its historic museum and art gallery. Antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Thomas Plant are searching the queue for the best antiques and collectibles to take to auction. Paul Martin explores the museum's world-class collection of pre-Raphaelite art and discovers the work of arts and crafts jewellery designers Arthur and Georgie Gaskin. Paul also visits Hagley Hall, the last great palladian house to be built in Britain.

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.desc: A rare medical condition meant Grace was unable to give birth, as she was born without a womb. But in a landmark moment for British medical history, her sister Amy made an extraordinary decision: to donate her womb to her sister in a complicated and involved operation, which lasted hours. Just months after the transplant, Grace became pregnant and now, with her husband, has a healthy daughter. It was a gift that transformed not only the sisters’ lives but those of everyone around them in a story of love, sacrifice and a deep family bond. Bill Bailey pairs Grace and Amy with Karen Turner, an award-winning artist with a powerful commitment to telling women’s stories. Karen works using an unusual and unforgiving technique, painting directly onto raw canvas. With no background layer and no possibility of correction, every paint stroke must be right. As Karen begins her work, she faces a profound challenge: how to capture the depth of love, trust and sacrifice that made this miracle possible. Can she find a way to express not just the medical achievement but the emotional truth behind it and create a portrait worthy of this remarkable act of sisterhood?

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Tough-talking Dom Littlewood and consumer champion Melinda Messenger turn their hand to antique dealing. Have Hampshire’s antique dealers met their match? In a break from hunting down cowboy builders and exposing wrongs, Dom and Melinda are in hot pursuit of profitable antiques. With £400 and a classic car, they’re paired with antiques experts Natasha Raskin Sharp and Tim Medhurst.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 15 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: The Top of the Pops vaults are opened up to relive some of the classic and biggest songs of 1992. Featuring the hip-hop and dance of Kris Kross, SL2, Bizarre Inc and Felix to the rock stylings of Manic Street Preachers and Primal Scream, as well as the emergence of boy band royalty Take That, Miley’s dad Billy Ray getting the line dancing going, divas in the form of Annie Lennox and Mariah Carey - and, of course, Jimmy Nail. Plus many more.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A look back at some of the songs performed on the show in 1993. Dance music and Euro pop continued to dominate the charts with performances from Haddaway, Sub Sub, Ace of Base and Robin S. The early stages of Britpop begin to emerge with Suede and Radiohead, and Icelandic superstar Björk makes an early appearance. Then there are the big sellers from Meat Loaf and Gabrielle, and the grooves come courtesy of Jamiroquai, Chaka Demus & Pliers and many more.

programme.title: Black Box
programme.desc: Obsessive forensic analyst Mathieu Vasseur is disappointed to be sidelined in the examination of the black box of an Atrian 800 airliner which has mysteriously crashed in the Alps. When his boss goes missing, Mathieu is asked to step in, and he concludes that there was an unauthorised entry into the cockpit. A press conference is held to announce the findings, but Mathieu is subsequently presented with conflicting evidence. French thriller with English subtitles.

programme.title: Numbri Aadmi
programme.desc: Numbri Aadmi is the story of an unknown Robinhood character who helps the poor and honest when they are threatened and harassed by powerful, ruthless and corrupt people. People call him as Numbri Aadmi, but they are unable to trace him, even though he lives among them.

programme.title: Numbri Aadmi
programme.desc: Numbri Aadmi is the story of an unknown Robinhood character who helps the poor and honest when they are threatened and harassed by powerful, ruthless and corrupt people. People call him as Numbri Aadmi, but they are unable to trace him, even though he lives among them.

programme.title: Brahmachari
programme.desc: Abandoned as a child, Brahmachari lives in a mortgaged house with twelve young orphans; he saves a woman from dying and she changes his life forever; but first she has to deal with her own affairs.

programme.title: Ghar Ka Chiraag
programme.desc: A wealthy widower marries the daughter of a school principal when her lover is assumed dead. Years later, her partner returns and sets out to find the truth behind her marriage.

programme.title: Aadmi
programme.desc: Vijay, a factory supervisor, is falsely accused and arrested for killing his family. Just when he is at his lowest point, an inspector offers to help him in return for capturing the son of a gangster.

programme.title: Apne Rang Hazaar
programme.desc: Sunil, a nonchalant playboy, loves flirting with girls. Soon, his life changes forever when his uncle gets murdered under mysterious circumstances.

programme.title: Ghar Ka Chiraag
programme.desc: A car racer injured and after recovery he find out that the love of his life no longer live. Years later he find out that she is a live and even has a son

programme.title: Lal Baadshah
programme.desc: Lal Singh lives alongside his mother in a small basti in Mumbai, where Vikram Singh is the don of the city and there is always clash between him and Lal Baadshah.

programme.title: Zamana
programme.desc: Ravi and Vinod's father is killed by a gangster for developing a photo that depicts a crime scene. Ravi, a criminal, and Vinod, a police officer, decide to exact revenge on their father's murderer.

programme.title: Be-Reham
